rulings.Case Title: D. P. OCHOA VS. CITY HOUSING, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday September 8, 2015, line 2. Defendant Mission Hotel and THC's Demurrer to Second Amended Complaint Sustained in part and Overruled in part. Pages 16-30 of Plaintiff's opposition are stricken. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the causes of action for negligence, nuisance and violation of statutory warranty as they are not addressed in Plaintiff's 30-page long opposition. Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the specific performance. Demurrer is sustained with a final leave to amend as to the causes of action for implied warranty of habitability, tortious breach of warranty of habitability and breach of covenant of quiet enjoyment for Plaintiff to allege facts supporting these causes of action not barred by statute of limitations. Demurrer is otherwise Overruled. It does not appear on the face of the complaint that the cause of action for Intentional Infliction for Emotional Distress is barred by statute of limitations, i.e. no specific move-out date is alleged in the Second Amended Complaint. Additionally, in sustaining the demurrer to the First Amended Complaint on the grounds of uncertainty the Court granted Plaintiff a broad leave to amend to separate out all the various causes of action asserted together under the headings of negligence and breach of contract. Newly asserted causes of action (2, 3, 5, 6 and 7) appear to be based on the facts alleged under those headings. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ESSEX MANAGEMENT CORPORATION, A CALIFORNIA VS. ANA ROBLERO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants' Responses Without Objection To Form Interrogatories General, Set One And Request For Monetary Sanctios
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday September 8, 2015, line 8. Plaintiff's Motion to Compel Responses to Discovery DENIED. Form Interrogatories - UD are specifically tailored to address elements of unlawful detainer cause of action and affirmative defenses. Form Interrogatories - General are not designed for use in unlawful detainer actions. Additionally, moving party failed to argue why responses to the questions regarding age, convictions, education, and employment are relevant in this non-payment of rent unlawful detainer. Further moving party fails to explain why responses to Form Interrogatory General 15 are necesary in addition to Form Interrogatories - UD pertaining to affirmative defenses. = (501/REQ)
